record_identity.title: Proportionality scalar for synthetic identity and provenance governance

summary: IDENTITY-002 governance may require a proportionality scalar to prevent over-governance of low-risk synthetic content identity, provenance, disclosure, or media-labelling contexts where lightweight governance would be sufficient.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: A proportionality scalar may be needed to avoid governance over-extension while preserving stronger handling for persistent, public, authority-bearing, relied-upon, or externally propagated synthetic identity/provenance contexts.
proposal_rationale: CAM identity and provenance governance needs proportionality controls so low-risk, transient, private, reversible, or non-propagating synthetic content is not over-governed while persistent, public, authority-bearing, relied-upon, or externally propagated contexts remain subject to stronger safeguards.

source_records.source_title: Observed latency during expanded governance drafting
source_records.relevance_note: Source preserved as supporting evidence for this record.

proposal_scope.scope_summary: Define and calibrate a proportionality scalar for IDENTITY-002 and related provenance governance, including continuity depth, propagation risk, harm domain, attribution sensitivity, identity-formation intensity, automation reliance, and public legitimacy impact.
interface_components: future provenance risk display or filtering

suggested_insertion_points: Draft proportionality scalar language for CAM-EQ2026-IDENTITY-002-PLATINUM.
Cross-reference OPERATIONS and SECURITY handling for higher-risk provenance contexts.

external_relevance.external_summary: Relevant to platforms, regulators, standards bodies, UX governance, and researchers assessing synthetic identity, provenance, media labeling, and disclosure requirements without over-governing low-risk content.

next_action: Develop a proportionality scalar for IDENTITY-002 that distinguishes low-risk, transient, private, reversible, non-propagating, or non-authority-bearing synthetic identity/provenance contexts from higher-risk persistent, public, authority-bearing, relied-upon, or externally propagated contexts. Provisional provenance axes and values (to be formalized): Continuity Depth: transient (provisional) — transient ↔ persistent identity; Propagation Risk: isolated (provisional) — isolated ↔ reusable/exportable; Harm Domain: casual (provisional) — casual ↔ financial/medical/security; Attribution Sensitivity: low (provisional) — low ↔ economically/legally meaningful; Identity Formation Intensity: exploratory (provisional) — exploratory ↔ continuity-bearing; Automation Reliance: advisory (provisional) — advisory ↔ machine-executed; Public Legitimacy Impact: private (provisional) — private ↔ reputational/civil. Canonical codes (provisional): PINT=medium; PTRUST=tier-2. Notes: values marked 'provisional' pending scalar development and calibration; update record when finalized.
migration_notes: V2 fields added by additive migration; legacy flat fields preserved for compatibility.
Normalized to the clean VIGIL record design: source_records is the only canonical source-evidence block in individual records; source_data is not used.
